required_skills: Qualitative Research Methods including Moderated Interviews, Contextual Inquiry, and Ethnographic Studies
Quantitative Research Methods including Surveys, Statistical Analysis, and Behavioral Data Interpretation
Usability Testing Design, Facilitation, and Analysis
Research Synthesis and Thematic Analysis using Affinity Mapping and Coding
Participant Recruitment, Screening, and Research Ethics Compliance
Research Repository Management and Insight Documentation
Persona, Mental Model, and Journey Map Development from Primary Research
Mixed-Methods Research Design and Study Planning
Experimental Design and Hypothesis Formulation for Concept Validation
preferred_skills: Eye-tracking, biometric, or physiological measurement research experience
Diary studies and longitudinal research methodology
Card sorting and tree testing for information architecture validation
Statistical software proficiency such as SPSS, R, or Python for data analysis
Survey design expertise using tools such as Qualtrics or SurveyMonkey
Experience with accessibility-focused user research with diverse and underrepresented populations
Familiarity with AI and machine learning product research challenges
Competitive benchmarking and UX scorecarding methodologies
frameworks: RITE Method (Rapid Iterative Testing and Evaluation)
responsibilities: Design and execute end-to-end mixed-methods research studies — from study planning and participant recruitment through facilitation, synthesis, and stakeholder readout — to inform product strategy and design decisions

Conduct in-depth qualitative interviews, contextual inquiries, and ethnographic field studies to uncover latent user needs, mental models, and behavioral patterns that quantitative data cannot surface alone

Design, launch, and analyze large-scale quantitative surveys and behavioral data studies to validate qualitative hypotheses and measure the prevalence of identified user needs across target populations

Facilitate moderated and unmoderated usability testing sessions on prototypes and live products using tools such as Maze, UserTesting, and Lookback to identify friction, confusion, and critical failure points
Synthesize raw research data into structured, actionable insights using affinity mapping, thematic coding, and journey mapping, producing artifacts that are accessible to non-researcher stakeholders
Manage and maintain a centralized research repository in tools such as Dovetail or Airtable, ensuring past insights are discoverable, tagged, and actively leveraged across the product organization

Collaborate with product managers and designers during discovery sprints to co-define research questions, scope studies appropriately, and integrate insights directly into roadmap and design decisions
Conduct card sorting, tree testing, and first-click testing studies using Optimal Workshop to validate and refine information architecture, navigation structures, and content labeling strategies

Communicate research findings clearly and persuasively to diverse audiences including designers, engineers, product managers, and C-suite executives through compelling written reports and live presentations
Champion ethical research practices including informed consent, participant privacy, data anonymization, and inclusive recruitment that reflects the full diversity of the product's user base
